content.cn_text: 叙利亚：嗨，格雷格。可再生能源的研究进展如何？你找到什么了吗？
content.en_text: Syria: Hi, Greg. How did it go with the research on renewable energy? Have you found anything?

content.cn_text: 格雷格：是的。但我认为是时候交流一下我们的发现，讨论一下下一步的行动了。
content.en_text: Greg: Yes. But I think it's about time we exchange our findings and discuss our next move.

content.cn_text: 叙利亚：你读懂了我的心思。正确的。我先开始。为了找到创造清洁能源的创新方法，我首先深入研究了德国，因为德国 15% 的电力供应来自可再生能源。我发现，除了传统的化石燃料行业，还有一家德国公司启动了一个利用风筝发电的项目。
content.en_text: Syria: You read my mind. Right. I'll start first. Germany is the very first country I dug into in order to find innovative means of creating clean energy because 15% of its national electricity supply comes from renewable sources. I found that apart from the traditional fossil fuel industry,there's a German firm that has initiated a project using kites to generate power.

content.cn_text: 格雷格：真的吗，我从来没听说过。它是如何工作的？
content.en_text: Greg: Really, I've never heard of it before. How does it work?

content.cn_text: 叙利亚：作为向大气中释放有毒气体的传统化石燃料的替代品，发电风筝可以在任何天气下工作。与传统的风力涡轮机相比，这种风筝可以产生两倍的能量，因为总功率密度与海拔成正比。
content.en_text: Syria: As a substitution for traditional fossil fuels that release toxic gas into the atmosphere,the power-generating kites can function in any weather. Compared to conventional wind turbines, such kites can produce twice as much energy because the overall power density is proportional to altitude.

content.cn_text: 格雷格：听起来是一种有效的发电方式。好吧，现在让我告诉你我发现了什么。有一家美国公司生产学校巴士和城市巴士，完全依靠电力而不是汽油。全电动汽车每天可以节省多达 20 加仑的燃料。这可以每年减少 1 万多美元的交通预算，更不用说节省的维修费用了。
content.en_text: Greg: Sounds like an efficient way of producing power. Okay, now let me tell you what I have found. There is an American company manufacturing school buses and city buses depending solely on electricity instead of gasoline. The all-electric vehicles can save up to 20 gallons of fuel on a daily basis. This could reduce transport budgets by over $10,000 each year, not to mention maintenance savings.

content.cn_text: 叙利亚：哇，令人印象深刻。要是周围有更多这样的电动汽车就好了。
content.en_text: Syria: Wow, impressive. If only there were more of these electric vehicles around.

content.cn_text: 格雷格：多年来，南非非常重视清洁能源。国家鼓励使用丙烷气，它既可以从自然沉积物中提取，也可以有机生产。它通常作为一种烹饪气体储存在气罐中。为减少厨房事故的发生，介绍了一种新型纤维复合气罐。它更安全，即使被火吞没也不太可能爆炸。
content.en_text: Greg: Well, over the years, South Africa has attached great importance to clean energy. The nation encourages using propane gas, which can either be extracted from natural deposits or be produced organically. It is normally stored in gas canisters as a type of cooking gas. To reduce the number of kitchen accidents, a new type of composite gas canister made of fiber was introduced. It is much safer and less likely to explode even when engulfed in fire.

content.cn_text: 格雷格：现在，关于调查，你知道我们应该包括什么样的受访者吗？
content.en_text: Greg: Now, about the survey, do you have any clues as to what kind of interviewees we should include?

content.cn_text: 叙利亚：当地从事清洁能源产品业务的公司情况如何？
content.en_text: Syria: How about local companies doing business on clean energy products?

content.cn_text: 格雷格：可能不是受访者的最佳选择。还记得上次我们让公司员工做问卷调查吗，只有 5% 的人愿意参与。
content.en_text: Greg: Probably not the best choice of respondents. Remember the last time we asked corporate employees to do the questionnaire, only about 5% of them were actually willing to participate.

content.cn_text: 叙利亚：那还不够。看来我们得放弃那个想法了。那么也许我们可以请这里的教授和行政人员来帮助我们。他们可以提供他们对能源生产的见解和理解。
content.en_text: Syria: That wouldn't be enough then. It seems we have to drop that idea. Then maybe we can ask the professors and administrative staff here to help us. They could provide their insights and understanding on energy production.

content.cn_text: 格雷格：如果他们愿意的话就再好不过了，但是恐怕他们中的大多数人都太忙了，无法回答我们准备的问题清单。我想大学里的学生会更合适。
content.en_text: Greg: It would be ideal if they would, but I'm afraid most of them are too busy to respond to the list of questions we've prepared. I guess the students here at the university would be more suitable.

content.cn_text: 叙利亚：你说得对。而且样本池也大得多。另外，我认为我们应该把当地人也包括进来。他们的意见是镇上推广可再生能源的关键。
content.en_text: Syria: You're right. And it is a much bigger sample pool too. Also, I think we should include the locals. Their opinion is key to the promotion of renewable energy here in the town.

content.cn_text: 格雷格：但是收集数据不是很困难吗？我们两个不可能挨家挨户去采访所有的住院医生。
content.en_text: Greg: But wouldn't it be difficult to collect data? There's no way the two of us could go from door to door to interview all the residents.

content.cn_text: 叙利亚：没有必要担心这个。我们将进行电话面试。这样我们就有足够的时间来获取足够的数据。
content.en_text: Syria: There's no need to worry about that. We'll make it telephone interviews. That way we'll have enough time to get sufficient data.

content.cn_text: 格雷格：好主意。我们应该在演讲中陈述什么？
content.en_text: Greg: Good idea. What should we present in our speech?

content.cn_text: 叙利亚：由于缺乏媒体报道，大多数人对可再生能源的了解实际上是有限的。他们中的大多数人无法识别各种类型的可再生能源。所以我觉得我们可以从澄清它是什么以及它与化石燃料相比的好处开始。
content.en_text: Syria: Due to lack of media coverage, the majority of people actually have a limited understanding on renewable energy. Most of them aren't able to identify various types of renewable sources. So I feel we could start by clarifying what it is and the benefit of it compared to fossil fuels.

content.cn_text: 格雷格：有道理。我们可以从风能开始。几个世纪以来，风一直被用来做工作。在风车的帮助下，农民过去常常从井里抽水或转动大磨石来磨小麦或玉米。今天的风车发电。
content.en_text: Greg: That makes sense. We could start with wind energy. For centuries, wind has been used to do work. With the help of windmills, farmers used to pump water from wells or turn large grinding stones to grind wheat or corn. The windmills today generate electricity.

content.cn_text: 唯一的问题是可能不会一直刮风，所以选择合适的地点建风电场是至关重要的。
content.en_text: The only problem is that it might not be windy all the time, so it is crucial to choose the appropriate site for wind farms.

content.cn_text: 叙利亚：嗯，我认为我们还可以将清洁能源与煤炭、石油和天然气等传统能源进行比较。
content.en_text: Syria: Well, I think we can also include comparisons between clean energy and traditional energy resources like coal, oil and natural gas.

content.cn_text: 格雷格：也许我们可以研究一下这些传统能源的前景。化石燃料价格的上涨和气候变化的威胁是许多人担心的问题
content.en_text: Greg: Maybe we can look into the prospect of these conventional sources of energy . The rising cost of fossil fuels and the threat of climate change is a concern to many

content.cn_text: 叙利亚：完全。这些传统资源最终会枯竭。可再生能源目前只占世界一次能源供应的不到 2%，尽管增长非常迅速，但它还不能填补化石燃料的空白。
content.en_text: Syria: Totally. These traditional resources will deplete eventually. Renewable energy currently makes up less than 2% of the world's primary energy supply, and although growing very rapidly, it is not on course to fill the fossil fuel gap.

content.cn_text: 格雷格：核能是我们应该提到的另一种能源。核电站可以持续可靠地发电，而且比其他传统发电厂排放的温室气体少得多。但是大多数人认为这种能量是不安全的，因为辐射不容易处理，尤其是在核废料和维护材料中。
content.en_text: Greg: Nuclear energy is another type of energy we ought to mention. Nuclear power plants can produce dependable power constantly and release far less greenhouse gases than other traditional power plants. But most people feel that this type of energy is unsafe because radiation isn't easily dealt with, especially in nuclear waste and maintenance materials.

content.cn_text: 叙利亚：我们应该用什么来结束演讲？
content.en_text: Syria: What should we end the speech with?

content.cn_text: 格雷格：你听说过一种叫做氢燃料的新能源吗？它是一种无限可再生的燃料，不会对环境产生有害影响。唯一的问题是，它太贵了，只有富人才能买得起。但我认为总的来说，它的好处掩盖了它的高成本。
content.en_text: Greg: Have you heard about a new type of energy called hydrogen fuel? It is an infinitely renewable fuel that doesn't have detrimental environmental effects. The only problem is that it is so expensive that only wealthy individuals can afford it. But I think overall the benefits overshadow its high cost.

content.cn_text: 叙利亚：我认为，尽管这种新型可再生能源目前使用起来过于昂贵，但从长远来看，它的价格会下降，变得更容易获得。
content.en_text: Syria: I think that even though this new type of renewable energy is too expensive to use at the moment, in the long run, its price will go down and become more accessible.

groups.desc:
Questions 21-23
Complete the table below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
Research about different energy-saving innovations

content: used in different$16470$conditions to reduce the emission of toxic gas
invented to reduce its chances to$16472$

items.analysis: 这道题的关键在于理解德国公司开发的风筝发电技术的特点。题目要求填写这种技术可以在什么条件下工作，而原文中明确提到"can function in any weather"。这里的"weather"就是我们需要的答案。这个信息突出了这种可再生能源技术的优势，即它可以在各种天气条件下工作，这使得它比传统的风力涡轮机更可靠。注意，虽然风筝通常与风有关，但这里强调的是它可以在任何天气条件下工作，而不是它依赖于风。理解这一点对于避免选择错误答案如"wind"很重要。此外，"weather"作为一个常见词，在听力中较容易识别，这也是出题者选择它作为答案的原因之一。

这道题的答案是"electricity"，因为原文明确提到美国公司生产的校车和城市公交车"depending solely on electricity instead of gasoline"（完全依靠电力而非汽油）。这里的"electricity"正是我们要找的创新能源。题目要求填写一个词，而"electricity"正好符合要求，既能体现节能创新，又与美国车辆相关。注意不要被"gasoline"误导，因为题目是关于节能创新，而不是传统能源。此外，"electricity"作为可再生能源，符合整篇文章讨论清洁能源的主题。所以，"electricity"是最合适的答案。

这道题的关键在于理解南非新型气罐的主要安全特征。文中提到这种新型复合气罐是为了减少厨房事故而引入的，并且特别强调了它"less likely to explode"（不太可能爆炸）这一特点。这里的"explode"（爆炸）就是我们需要填入的答案。题目要求我们填写这种气罐被发明是为了减少什么的可能性，显然，根据原文，减少的是爆炸的可能性。因此，"explode"是最合适的答案。注意，虽然文中也提到了"fire"（火），但题目要求填写的是一个动词，而"fire"是名词，所以不能作为答案。这道题考察了你对关键信息的捕捉能力和对上下文的理解能力。

groups.title: Which TWO types of interviewees do Greg and Syria choose to do the survey?

collect_options.title: Which TWO types of interviewees do Greg and Syria choose to do the survey?

items.analysis: 根据对话内容，Greg和Syria最终选择了大学生和当地居民作为调查对象。他们认为大学生更适合回答问题，而当地居民的意见对于推广可再生能源至关重要。他们排除了其他选项，如教授和行政人员（因为太忙），以及公司员工（因为参与率低）。选择大学生和当地居民既能确保足够的样本量，又能获得有价值的意见。
根据对话内容，Greg和Syria最终选择了大学生和当地居民作为调查对象。他们认为大学生更适合回答问题，而当地居民的意见对于推广可再生能源至关重要。他们排除了其他选项，如教授和行政人员（因为太忙），以及公司员工（因为参与率低）。选择大学生和当地居民既能确保足够的样本量，又能获得有价值的意见。

groups.desc:
Questions 26-30
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

items.title: What does Syria think about renewable energy?

options.title: A
options.content: Most people know very little about renewable energy.

options.title: B
options.content: The general public can distinguish different kinds of renewable energy.

options.title: C
options.content: The majority of people feel the need to replace fossil fuels with renewable energy.

items.analysis: Syria认为大多数人对可再生能源知之甚少，这一观点在原文中得到了明确表述。她指出由于缺乏媒体报道，大多数人对可再生能源的了解有限，甚至无法识别不同类型的可再生能源。这直接对应了选项A的描述，即大多数人对可再生能源知之甚少。

items.title: What do the speakers say about modem windmills?

options.title: A
options.content: They can be used to pump water from wells.

options.title: B
options.content: The location to build them should be carefully chosen.

options.title: C
options.content: Farmers use them to grind grain.

items.analysis: 文中提到现代风车（windmills today）用于发电，但指出了一个关键问题：风力并非始终存在。因此，选择合适的地点对于风力发电场来说至关重要。这直接对应了选项B，强调了选址的重要性。现代风车的有效运作依赖于正确的位置选择，这是题目所询问的关键信息。

items.title: What aspect of traditional fuels are they going to address?

options.title: A
options.content: how to avoid fossil fuels from being depleted

options.title: B
options.content: the possible future of traditional resources

options.title: C
options.content: how to prevent pollution of traditional resources

items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B，因为原文中Greg提到"Maybe we can look into the prospect of these conventional sources of energy"，其中"prospect"（前景）一词直接对应了选项B中的"possible future"（可能的未来）。此外，紧接着提到的"rising cost of fossil fuels and the threat of climate change"也进一步强调了对传统能源未来的讨论。

items.title: What does Greg say about nuclear plants?

options.title: A
options.content: They are cleaner and less expensive than fossil fuel.

options.title: B
options.content: It is the best way to fight climate change.

options.title: C
options.content: Few people think they are safe

items.analysis: 文中明确指出"most people feel that this type of energy is unsafe"，这直接对应了正确答案C "Few people think they are safe"。尽管核电站实际上能够稳定发电并减少温室气体排放，但大多数人仍然认为它不安全，主要是因为辐射问题难以处理。这个观点强调了公众对核能安全性的普遍担忧，而不是核能的实际效果或优势。

items.title: What does Syria think of hydrogen fuel?

options.title: A
options.content: The price of it will drop eventually.

options.title: C
options.content: The benefits of it overweigh its high cost.

items.analysis: Syria认为氢燃料虽然目前价格昂贵，但长期来看价格会下降。这直接对应了A选项"The price of it will drop eventually"。Syria的观点强调了氢燃料价格的长期趋势，而不是当前的成本效益比较或环境问题。因此，A选项最准确地反映了Syria对氢燃料的看法。
